title: Financial Action Task Force
summary: This page provides a general overview of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), an international body created by the G-7 in 1989 to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. The U.S. delegation to FATF is led by Treasury staff and includes representatives from State, Justice, the National Security Council, and financial regulators.
key_points: FATF is an international policy-making and standard-setting body created by the G-7 in 1989
The organization monitors members' progress in implementing anti-money laundering measures
The U.S. delegation is chaired by Treasury staff and includes State, Justice, NSC, and federal regulators
The delegation develops U.S. positions and represents the U.S. at FATF meetings
No specific policy decisions, data, or actionable information is provided on this page
why_it_matters: This page is not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts. It is an informational/background page describing the FATF organization's structure and mission. It does not contain economic data, policy decisions, rate announcements, or market-moving information.
